title: [apiv2] curl will hang forever when sent a signal query parameters for Kill a container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &

2. start a container

3.  curl -v -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/romantic_hellman/kill?signal=TERM"

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED             STATUS                 PORTS   NAMES
24490537bc2c  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  /bin/sh  About a minute ago  Up About a minute ago          romantic_hellman

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$  curl -v -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/romantic_hellman/kill?signal=TERM"
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/romantic_hellman/kill?signal=TERM HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-Type: application/json
>                                                     <---------- no any response

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ps axuf|grep curl
fedora    161374  0.0  0.1  90132  7816 pts/2    S+   08:44   0:00  |           \_ cur  -v -X POST -H Content-Type: application/json http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/romantic_hellman/kill?signal=TERM

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ strace -p 161374
strace: Process 161374 attached
restart_syscall(<... resuming interrupted read ...>) = 0
rt_sigaction(SIGPIPE, NULL, {sa_handler=SIG_IGN, sa_mask=[PIPE], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7efe8395da90}, 8) = 0
rt_sigaction(SIGPIPE, {sa_handler=SIG_IGN, sa_mask=[PIPE], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7efe8395da90}, NULL, 8) = 0
poll([{fd=6, events=POLLIN|POLLPRI|POLLRDNORM|POLLRDBAND}], 1, 0) = 0 (Timeout)
rt_sigaction(SIGPIPE, {sa_handler=SIG_IGN, sa_mask=[PIPE], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7efe8395da90}, NULL, 8) = 0
poll([{fd=6, events=POLLIN}, {fd=3, events=POLLIN}], 2, 1000) = 0 (Timeout)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Fix it

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
It worked on the following method.

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
491bc6eabf89  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  /bin/sh  8 minutes ago  Up 8 minutes ago          kind_rosalind

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$  curl -v -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"signal":"TERM"}' "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/kind_rosalind/kill"
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/kind_rosalind/kill HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
> Content-Type: application/json
> Content-Length: 17
>
* upload completely sent off: 17 out of 17 bytes
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
< Date: Fri, 29 May 2020 08:33:09 GMT
<
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ echo $?
0

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
491bc6eabf89  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  /bin/sh  7 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago          kind_rosalind
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.0-dev
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.2
Git Commit:   cd1e25f5d57059a50e8aeae5b640c2d282e70f96
Built:        Fri May 29 08:07:14 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.0-dev
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.15-1.fc32.x86_64

  cpus: 4

  eventLogger: file

  idMappings:

      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.6.10-300.fc32.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1726865408
  memTotal: 4118323200
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.0.0-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.0
      commit: a3be729152a33e692cd28b52f664defbf2e7810a
      libslirp: 4.2.0
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 432h 42m 24.22s (Approximately 18.00 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com

  configFile: /home/fedora/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2

  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1

        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/fedora/.local/share/containers/storage
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

cd1e25f5d57059a50e8aeae5b640c2d282e70f96
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openstack VM

comments.body:
I tried all three of these invocations and each worked.

```
sudo curl -v -X POST --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?SIGNAL=TERM'
sudo curl -v -X POST --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?signal=TERM'
sudo curl -v -X POST --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?signal=term'
```
The container I used was"

```
sudo podman run -dt --name baude alpine top
```

Could you provide a repro for the container you ran?

> ```
> sudo curl -v -X POST --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?signal=term'
> ```

I hadn't found podman.sock on my local host

> The container I used was"
> 
> ```
> sudo podman run -dt --name baude alpine top
> ```
> 
> Could you provide a repro for the container you ran?

podman run -td alpine  /bin/sh
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't believe this is a bug, Can I close it?

It doesn't work for me w/ upstream in rootless mode, it may be a configuration issue in podman.socket or something like that, because I can successfully kill a container w/ Baude's steps by downstream podman-1.9.3-2.module+el8.2.1+6867+366c07d6.x86_64.

Basically, I followed contrib/systemd/README.md to start podman.socket in rootless mode

```shell
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/home/fedora/.config/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Tue 2020-07-07 09:31:24 UTC; 12s ago
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/podman.socket

Jul 07 09:31:24 fedora32 systemd[1644]: Listening on Podman API Socket.

● podman.service - Podman API Service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/fedora/.config/systemd/user/podman.service; disabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: inactive (dead)
TriggeredBy: ● podman.socket
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman run -dt --name baude alpine top
43dc75355a05031a13337383c92b144842d1f1fcf483e09b1da35988d37ac13a

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED        STATUS            PORTS   NAMES
43dc75355a05  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  top      4 seconds ago  Up 3 seconds ago          baude

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -X POST --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?SIGNAL=TERM'
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to d (/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> POST /v1.40/libpod/containers/baude/kill?SIGNAL=TERM HTTP/1.1
> Host: d
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
* Closing connection 0
curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED             STATUS                 PORTS   NAMES
43dc75355a05  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  top      About a minute ago  Up About a minute ago          baude

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/home/fedora/.config/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: failed (Result: trigger-limit-hit) since Tue 2020-07-07 09:33:58 UTC; 3min 9s ago
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)
     Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Jul 07 09:31:24 fedora32 systemd[1644]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
Jul 07 09:33:58 fedora32 systemd[1644]: podman.socket: Trigger limit hit, refusing further activation.
Jul 07 09:33:58 fedora32 systemd[1644]: podman.socket: Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit'.

● podman.service - Podman API Service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/fedora/.config/systemd/user/podman.service; disabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Tue 2020-07-07 09:33:58 UTC; 3min 9s ago
TriggeredBy: ● podman.socket
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)
    Process: 85896 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman system service (code=exited, status=203/EXEC)
   Main PID: 85896 (code=exited, status=203/EXEC)
        CPU: 1ms

Jul 07 09:33:58 fedora32 systemd[1644]: Starting Podman API Service...

Jul 07 09:33:58 fedora32 systemd[1644]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=203/EXEC

```

NOTE: it still doesn't work when tried to sudo make install or sudo copy ./bin/podman /usr/bin/podman or modified podman.service to point to local podman binary.

This works for me in rootless mode with podman.socket installed via `podman-2.0.3-1.fc32.x86_64` as well as `./bin/podman system service` from upstream master.

But I can see the issue with `./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &`. I guess that one is supposed to work as well, yes? :)
@lsm5 When you started the service on the tcp port, what was the curl command you used?

> @lsm5 When you started the service on the tcp port, what was the curl command you used?

```
curl -v -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" "http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/containers/romantic_hellman/kill?signal=TERM"
```
but with the container name changed of course. It worked just fine without `?signal=TERM`
@lsm5 I did some debugging. What is the value of --time in your ~/.config/systemd/user/podman.service file?

> @lsm5 I did some debugging. What is the value of --time in your ~/.config/systemd/user/podman.service file?

Hmm, no such file here. Does it consider `/usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.service` then?
this seems to work as expected with signal=TERM on latest master.

> this seems to work as expected with signal=TERM on latest master.

I tested also can work normally
